skill.name: journal-cover-prompter
skill.description: Generates detailed AI art prompts for scientific journal cover images and graphical abstracts. Supports multiple art styles, color palettes, and journal-specific formatting for publication-quality visuals.
skill.summary: Create professional AI image prompts for scientific journal covers and graphical abstracts
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 59 static analyzer findings are false positives. The skill is a Python prompt generator for scientific journal cover artwork with no network access, no file system operations, and no cryptographic code. Markdown backtick formatting was misidentified as shell execution, SHA256 hash metadata in the audit JSON was misidentified as weak cryptographic algorithms, audit terminology was misidentified as system reconnaissance, and a documentation source URL was misidentified as a hardcoded network endpoint. The underlying code is safe and the skill performs its documented function without any security risk.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown backticks flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: 33 locations in SKILL.md and references/audit-reference.md flagged as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All instances are standard markdown inline code formatting (backticks around file paths, command names, and code snippets). No actual shell execution via backticks is present in either file. The files are documentation, not executable code.

low_findings.title: False positive: Hex hash values flagged as weak cryptographic algorithms
low_findings.description: 24 locations across all four files flagged as weak cryptographic algorithms. The audit JSON contains SHA256 hash metadata values (content_hash, tree_hash) which are hex strings, not cryptographic implementations. SKILL.md contains YAML frontmatter and description text with no crypto code. scripts/main.py contains docstrings and argparse setup, not cryptography. No cryptographic algorithm is implemented anywhere in this skill.

low_findings.title: False positive: Source repository URL flagged as hardcoded URL
low_findings.description: Line 7 of SKILL.md contains the GitHub source URL for the skill repository in a markdown blockquote. This is a documentation reference, not a network request. The skill makes no HTTP calls, fetch operations, or any network connections.

low_findings.title: False positive: Audit terminology flagged as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: 6 locations across the audit JSON and SKILL.md flagged as system reconnaissance. The audit JSON contains terms like execution_mode and command exit code as metadata fields in an audit report. SKILL.md section headers like Quick Check and audit-ready command documentation are flagged. No system information gathering or reconnaissance code exists in this skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown backtick formatting (false positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged 33 markdown backtick characters as shell execution. These are standard markdown inline code formatting characters used to denote code spans, file paths, and command names in documentation. No shell execution via backticks is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: SHA256 hash hex strings (false positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged SHA256 hexadecimal hash values in the audit JSON and plain text as weak cryptographic algorithms. These are hash metadata values (content_hash, tree_hash) used for file integrity verification, not cryptographic algorithm implementations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documentation source URL (false positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: The GitHub repository URL in SKILL.md line 7 is a documentation reference to the skill's source code location within a markdown blockquote. This is not a network call, fetch operation, or data exfiltration endpoint.

dangerous_patterns.title: Audit report metadata fields (false positive)
dangerous_patterns.description: Terms like execution_mode and command_exit_code in the audit JSON are metadata fields documenting test execution results. Section headers like Quick Check and audit-ready commands in SKILL.md are documentation. No system reconnaissance code exists.

content.user_title: Generate Scientific Journal Cover Art Prompts
content.value_statement: Researchers and academics struggle to create effective prompts for AI image generators when designing journal cover art. This skill generates detailed, publication-ready prompts with scientific accuracy, appropriate art styles, and journal-specific formatting.
actual_capabilities: Generates AI art prompts for scientific journal cover images with customizable research topics
Supports multiple art styles including photorealistic, artistic, minimalist, dramatic, and abstract
Provides mood and atmosphere options such as innovative, hopeful, mysterious, powerful, and serene
Includes color palette selection with scientific-themed options like blue, green, red, purple, warm, cool, and rainbow
Outputs technical specifications including aspect ratio, DPI, and print quality requirements
Produces negative prompts and suggested AI tools for refined image generation
limitations: Does not generate actual images, only text prompts for external AI image generators
Prompt quality depends on the clarity and specificity of the user-provided research topic
Style and mood options are limited to predefined categories and cannot be fully customized
No integration with specific image generation APIs such as DALL-E or Midjourney

use_cases.title: Journal Cover Submission
use_cases.description: A researcher preparing a manuscript for Nature or Cell generates a professional cover art prompt that matches the journal's visual style and their research topic.

use_cases.title: Graphical Abstract Creation
use_cases.description: A PhD student creates a visually compelling graphical abstract prompt to accompany their paper submission, improving reader engagement and comprehension.

use_cases.title: Grant Proposal Visuals
use_cases.description: A principal investigator generates scientific artwork prompts for grant proposal figures and presentations to funding agencies, enhancing visual communication of research goals.

prompt_templates.title: Basic cover art prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a journal cover art prompt for my research topic: CRISPR gene editing for cancer therapy. Use a photorealistic style with a hopeful mood.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user with a clear research topic who needs a straightforward cover prompt

prompt_templates.title: Style-specific prompt with elements
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a scientific journal cover prompt for Nature-style publication. Topic: synaptic transmission in neurodegenerative disease. Style: dramatic. Key elements: neurons, synapses, glowing connections, dark background. Color palette: purple and blue.
prompt_templates.scenario: User targeting a specific journal who has visual elements in mind

prompt_templates.title: Full specification prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a journal cover art prompt for my paper on quantum computing error correction. Journal: Science. Style: abstract futuristic. Mood: powerful and innovative. Colors: cool tones with accent gold. Exclude text and labels. The image should convey stability emerging from complexity.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user who wants full control over all prompt parameters

prompt_templates.title: Comparative style exploration
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate three different cover art prompts for the same research topic: immunotherapy response in melanoma patients. Version 1: photorealistic clinical style. Version 2: artistic abstract representation. Version 3: minimalist clean design. Show me the differences so I can compare.
prompt_templates.scenario: User exploring multiple visual approaches before selecting a direction

output_examples.input: Generate a journal cover prompt for research on nanoparticle drug delivery systems
output_examples.output: Scientific journal cover art depicting nanoparticle drug delivery systems, dramatic lighting, cinematic, high contrast, innovative, cutting-edge, futuristic, breakthrough, color palette: deep blue, cyan, scientific blue, high resolution, professional quality, suitable for journal cover. Technical: 16:9 aspect ratio, 300 DPI, print quality. Avoid: cluttered, low quality, cartoonish.

output_examples.input: Create a Nature-style cover prompt for neuroscience research on memory formation
output_examples.output: Scientific journal cover art depicting memory formation in the brain, artistic interpretation, stylized, visually striking, mysterious, intriguing, discovery, unknown, color palette: violet, scientific purple, rich, high resolution, professional quality, suitable for journal cover. Technical: 16:9 aspect ratio, 300 DPI, print quality. Avoid: cluttered, low quality, cartoonish.

output_examples.input: Design a minimalist cover prompt for stem cell regeneration research
output_examples.output: Scientific journal cover art depicting stem cell regeneration, clean, minimal, elegant, focused composition, hopeful, optimistic, healing, life-saving, color palette: emerald, teal, life sciences green, high resolution, professional quality, suitable for journal cover. Technical: 16:9 aspect ratio, 300 DPI, print quality. Avoid: cluttered, low quality, cartoonish.

best_practices: Provide a specific and well-defined research topic rather than a broad field name for better prompt quality
Match the art style and mood to your target journal conventions for higher acceptance chances
Review the generated prompt before submitting to an AI image generator and adjust scientific accuracy as needed
anti_patterns: Using vague research descriptions such as biology or chemistry without specific mechanisms or concepts
Selecting a mood that contradicts the tone of your research findings such as hopeful for a study about disease progression
Expecting the skill to generate actual images rather than text prompts for external AI image generators

faq.question: What does this skill actually produce?
faq.answer: This skill generates detailed text prompts that you can copy and paste into AI image generators such as Midjourney, DALL-E 3, or Stable Diffusion to create journal cover artwork.

faq.question: Which image generators work with these prompts?
faq.answer: The prompts are compatible with all major AI image generators including Midjourney, DALL-E 3, Stable Diffusion, and Adobe Firefly.

faq.question: Can I customize the output for a specific journal?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports journal-specific style guidance for Nature, Cell, Science, and medical journals, each with appropriate visual conventions.

faq.question: Does this skill require any special software?
faq.answer: The skill runs in Python 3.10 or later with no external dependencies. It only uses the Python standard library.

faq.question: Can I generate prompts in batch for multiple research topics?
faq.answer: The CLI accepts one topic at a time. For batch generation, you can script multiple calls or run the skill repeatedly with different inputs.

faq.question: How do I know if my prompt will produce a good image?
faq.answer: Review the generated prompt for scientific accuracy and visual clarity. The skill includes tips for each prompt and suggests avoiding cluttered or cartoonish results.
